title: magnolia acuminata subsp. cordata
intro: Magnolia acuminata subsp. cordata, also known as the heartleaf yellow-poplar, is a subspecies of the large deciduous tree, Magnolia acuminata, native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Magnolia acuminata subsp. cordata include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol and blood pressure. Magnolia acuminata subsp. cordata can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
